vehicles.exclusionReason: Kia remains excluded as a live candidate; retained only as the performance, dashboard and ownership benchmark.
vehicles.availabilityUk: Benchmark only; current-model pricing and lease visibility deliberately not used for candidacy.

facts.audio: Meridian system in the prior benchmark vehicle.
facts.dashboard: Integrated curved dual-screen layout remains the positive ergonomics benchmark.
facts.phoneIntegration: Known personal benchmark; current-model reliability not refreshed.
facts.security: Theft experience makes it a negative security benchmark; current mitigations not scored.

notes: Reference vehicle only; not eligible for shortlist promotion.
Use its integrated display, acceleration and prior ownership experience as comparison points.
sources: Personal benchmark and established EV6 specification references.
Benchmark-only entry rechecked 2026-07-24; no candidacy research performed.

vehicles.availabilityUk: On sale in the UK for model year 2027 from £33,060. Volvo lists P8 AWD at up to 279.6 miles WLTP; affected earlier cars still require recall/VIN verification.

facts.audio: Harman Kardon soundbar system is available on higher trims.
facts.dashboard: Single 12.3-inch central display with very limited physical controls; major fit risk.
facts.phoneIntegration: Google built-in and wireless Apple CarPlay. Volvo says forthcoming free OTA updates will introduce a more intuitive UX, but real-world display/software reliability remains a concern.
facts.security: Five-star crash safety, but theft/insurance/keyless mitigation evidence remains incomplete.

flags.featureSubscription: Google digital services are included for a limited period, then renewal terms may apply
flags.qualityConcern: UK battery-overheat recall affects 10,577 vehicles; affected cars must remain at or below 70% until repaired

notes: No status movement. Model-year updates, V2L and promised free UX improvements are positives, but they do not solve the central-screen/minimal-control mismatch.
The battery-recall/VIN check remains mandatory for any earlier-stock vehicle.
Still not worth prioritising for a test drive unless a dealer can demonstrate repaired recall status and current software stability.
sources: Volvo UK recall information for EX30 R10360.
GOV.UK recall R/2025/559, published 22 January 2026.
Volvo UK EX30 software release notes.
Euro NCAP EX30 2024 result.
Volvo Cars UK EX30 MY27 page and July 2026 product update, checked 2026-07-24.

vehicles.origin: US brand, Europe-built, VW MEB platform
vehicles.availabilityUk: On sale with strong UK retail and salary-sacrifice support. The £1,500 Electric Car Grant explicitly covers selected Style/Select Extended Range versions; Ford also advertises a £1,000 customer saving, 0% APR on eligible versions and Power Promise support to 30 September 2026.

facts.audio: Optional 10-speaker B&O system with subwoofer and integrated soundbar.
facts.dashboard: 14.6-inch tilting portrait screen plus a separate 5.3-inch driver display; still screen-led but less crude than a freestanding tablet.
facts.phoneIntegration: Wireless smartphone integration is offered; reconnect reliability still lacks strong owner evidence.
facts.security: Five-star crash rating; theft resistance, keyless controls and tracker requirements remain unverified.

flags.grantEligibility: Confirmed for 26.75MY Explorer Style RWD Extended Range and Select RWD Extended Range; Premium/B&O configuration and finance exclusions must be checked against the exact quote.

notes: Moves up on value evidence, not formal status. Ford now confirms the grant on selected Extended Range trims and says it is usable through leases/company-car schemes including salary sacrifice.
Current retail promotion also includes a £1,000 customer saving, 0% APR on eligible versions and Ford Power Promise; exact exclusions matter.
Ready for a quote on an Extended Range car with B&O, but performance remains merely adequate rather than exciting.
sources: Ford UK Explorer model page and promotions, checked 2026-07-17.
Ford UK price list effective 1 April 2026.
Euro NCAP Ford Explorer 2024 result.
Ford UK Explorer page, promotions and EV-grant guidance, checked 2026-07-24.

vehicles.availabilityUk: On sale with test-drive visibility. Smart advertises 0% APR across the range and up to £2,350 in manufacturer offers on #5 model lines through 30 September 2026; confirm the BRABUS-specific amount.

facts.audio: 20-speaker Sennheiser system with Dolby Atmos support.
facts.dashboard: Multi-screen hyperscreen-style fascia; visually integrated but highly software-dependent.
facts.phoneIntegration: CarPlay/Android Auto support is offered, but UK owner-level reconnect evidence remains thin.
facts.security: Five-star Euro NCAP; theft, app security, insurance and tracker evidence remain incomplete.

flags.featureSubscription: Premium Connectivity Package is listed at £84 per 12 months and covers OTA maps, in-car apps and online services; third-party streaming subscriptions may also be required
flags.weakUkSupport: dealer network exists but model/software maturity is still young
flags.currentPromotion: 0% APR and up to £2,350 manufacturer support on smart #5 model lines; BRABUS-specific support is not stated in the evidence.

notes: Remains the strongest overall test-drive candidate. The current 0% APR and #5 manufacturer support improve the value case, subject to BRABUS-specific confirmation.
The £84 annual Premium Connectivity Package remains a concrete subscription red flag.
The test drive must still stress low-speed ride, repeated phone reconnection, common controls, ADAS warnings, insurance and keyless security.
sources: Smart UK #5 and BRABUS specifications, checked 2026-07-17.
Smart UK digital-products store for Premium Connectivity Package pricing.
Euro NCAP Smart #5 2025 result.
Recent European road tests on comfort, materials and charging.
Smart UK #5 specifications, digital store and summer promotions, checked 2026-07-24.

vehicles.availabilityUk: On sale from £63,365. Current retail support offers either a complimentary Ohme charger or £500 off; this does not materially resolve the budget problem.

facts.audio: Bang & Olufsen 3D audio with head-rest speakers is pack dependent.
facts.dashboard: Integrated curved OLED panoramic display; premium but screen-heavy. Avoid virtual mirrors unless personally acceptable.
facts.phoneIntegration: Android Automotive platform with Apple CarPlay/Android Auto; reliability still needs owner evidence.
facts.security: Five-star crash rating; theft, keyless and insurer/tracker requirements still need direct confirmation.

flags.featureSubscription: verify Audi connected-feature and navigation renewal terms

notes: No status movement. The product fit remains strong, but current retail support is too small to overcome the purchase-price ceiling.
Only an unusually favourable salary-sacrifice quote can move it back to the shortlist.
Any dealer enquiry should specify B&O, conventional mirrors and a comfort-oriented wheel/suspension configuration.
sources: Audi UK A6 e-tron model information.
Euro NCAP A6 e-tron 2025 result.
Current UK pricing and road-test evidence rechecked 2026-07-17.
Audi UK A6 Sportback e-tron finance and offers page, checked 2026-07-24.

vehicles.availabilityUk: UK-configurable with official Personal/Business Contract Hire and finance options. Volvo advertises 3.9% APR plus £1,500 contribution, or 0% Volvo Loan with 50% deposit, and a complimentary Ohme charger. A current stock Ultra Twin Motor example was listed at about £987/month PCP.

facts.audio: Bose on Plus and 25-speaker Bowers & Wilkins with Dolby Atmos on Ultra.
facts.dashboard: Large central touchscreen with a small driver display; more premium than EX30 but still strongly screen-led.
facts.phoneIntegration: Google built-in and wireless Apple CarPlay; long-term software reliability is not yet established.
facts.security: Extensive sensor suite, but no completed Euro NCAP result found and theft/insurance evidence is immature.
facts.euroNcap: Not yet confirmed in current evidence.

flags.featureSubscription: Google services renewal terms may apply after the included period
flags.leaseVisibility: Official PCH/BCH availability is now confirmed, but observed Ultra Twin Motor pricing remains far outside the target value envelope.

notes: Lease availability is no longer an evidence gap, but the available evidence makes the affordability problem clearer rather than better.
The 25-speaker Bowers & Wilkins system, quiet cabin and charging remain highly attractive; price and missing Euro NCAP confirmation still block progression.
Do not prioritise a test drive unless a salary-sacrifice quote is unexpectedly close to the Smart/Cupra alternatives.
sources: Volvo UK ES90 model information.
Recent road tests praising the 25-speaker Bowers & Wilkins system and comfort.
Euro NCAP database rechecked 2026-07-17; no confirmed result retained.
Volvo UK ES90 model, offers and live stock pages, checked 2026-07-24.

vehicles.availabilityUk: On sale with strong dealer and finance visibility. V2 is £44,495 and includes the 12-speaker Sennheiser system; VZ1 remains £54,930 and VZ2 £59,830.

facts.audio: The 12-speaker Sennheiser system is standard on VZ2; official VZ2 pricing is £59,830, above budget.
facts.dashboard: Driver-oriented cockpit with a prominent central display and divisive centre spine.
facts.phoneIntegration: Apple CarPlay/Android Auto available; reconnect reliability still needs owner validation.
facts.security: Five-star Euro NCAP; Kessy keyless entry and a mobile digital key are offered, but theft/insurance evidence remains unverified.

flags.featureSubscription: verify Cupra Connect renewal terms and feature policy
flags.theftSecurityConcern: Kessy and beta mobile digital key require direct insurance/security validation
flags.expensiveAudioTrim: VZ2 with Sennheiser is £59,830, above the £55k ceiling
flags.alternativeTrim: V2 at £44,495 includes Sennheiser and is the value/audio alternative; it gives up the VZ dual-motor performance and must be compared directly.
flags.currentPromotion: 0% APR or £7,750 finance-deposit contribution on current official Tavascan offers, ordered by 2 August 2026.

notes: Moves up in practical consideration because V2 resolves the previous audio-versus-budget conflict: £44,495 with Sennheiser.
The trade-off is performance. Test V2 and VZ back-to-back rather than assuming the faster VZ is automatically the better fit.
Current 0% APR / deposit-contribution support strengthens the dealer-enquiry case, but salary-sacrifice pricing remains the relevant comparison.
sources: Cupra UK Tavascan 2026 configurator and trim pricing, checked 2026-07-17.
Euro NCAP Tavascan 2024 result.
Current UK lease visibility.
Cupra UK Tavascan range and PCP offers, checked 2026-07-24.

vehicles.availabilityUk: New 2026 Born VZ is on sale from £45,995 with established dealer, test-drive and salary-sacrifice visibility.

facts.audio: Sennheiser AMBEO immersive sound system is standard on VZ.
facts.dashboard: 10.25-inch driver display, 12.9-inch Android-based infotainment and mechanical steering-wheel controls; materially better aligned with the control preference.
facts.phoneIntegration: Google/Apple Maps can be shown in the driver display; My Cupra app and phone integration are offered, but reconnect reliability still needs testing.
facts.security: Five-star Euro NCAP rating from the existing model generation; keyless/theft evidence still incomplete.

flags.featureSubscription: Verify My Cupra/Cupra Connect renewal terms; no hardware comfort-feature subscription found.
flags.newModelSoftware: New Android-based infotainment needs real-world reliability validation.

notes: Promoted to shortlist. The 2026 VZ now combines £45,995 pricing, standard Sennheiser audio, 326 PS, 5.6-second acceleration, up to 388 miles and improved mechanical steering-wheel controls.
It is less dramatic than the Smart #5 Brabus but currently looks like the strongest balanced/value alternative.
Book a test drive focused on ride comfort, Sennheiser quality, Android infotainment stability, phone reconnection, keyless security and road-bike practicality.
sources: Cupra Born VZ specifications and current UK market listings.
Euro NCAP Cupra Born result.
Current UK lease visibility rechecked 2026-07-17.
Cupra UK New Born 2026 model page and current offers, checked 2026-07-24.

vehicles.availabilityUk: On sale through the dedicated UK MG IM site with configurator and test-drive booking.

facts.audio: Official UK site confirms a 20-speaker system, double-layer glass and road-noise cancelling technology.
facts.dashboard: 26.3-inch panoramic display plus central screen; very high ergonomics risk for this shortlist.
facts.phoneIntegration: Official UK site confirms wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto across the 26.3-inch display; reconnect/app reliability remains unproven.
facts.security: Five-star Euro NCAP is now confirmed; theft, insurance, keyless and app-security evidence remains insufficient.

flags.touchOnlyBasicControls: high risk; reviews report mirror and other basic adjustments buried in menus
flags.theftSecurityConcern: high uncertainty despite five-star crash rating
flags.weakUkSupport: MG dealer network exists, but IM software/support maturity is unproven
flags.adasIntrusiveness: independent review evidence reports excessive and confusing safety alerts
flags.officialConnectivityConfirmed: Wireless CarPlay/Android Auto confirmed; reliability is still an evidence gap.

notes: Moves up only in evidence quality: official UK material now confirms the 20-speaker system, wireless CarPlay/Android Auto, configurator and test-drive availability.
It remains research-only because screen dependence, intrusive ADAS reports, UK software maturity, insurance and theft resistance are not proven strongly enough.
Suitable for a research-oriented demonstration, not for ordering.
sources: Euro NCAP IM5 2025 result: 89% adult, 85% child, 85% vulnerable-road-user and 87% safety-assist scores.

Official MG IM5 UK model, configurator and test-drive pages, checked 2026-07-24.

facts.audio: 20-speaker system is presented as part of the UK IM experience; exact trim inclusion still needs confirmation.
facts.dashboard: 26.3-inch panoramic display plus central screen; very high ergonomics risk.
facts.phoneIntegration: Official UK site confirms wireless Apple CarPlay and Android Auto; reconnect/app reliability remains unproven.
facts.security: Five-star Euro NCAP is a positive; theft, insurance, keyless and app-security evidence remains incomplete.

flags.theftSecurityConcern: high uncertainty despite five-star crash rating

notes: Moves up only in evidence quality: the UK site confirms 751 hp, 3.5-second acceleration, up to 388 miles, wireless CarPlay/Android Auto and active test-drive availability.
Air suspension/four-wheel steering and cabin quietness make a dealer demonstration worthwhile, but screen dependence, security, insurance and long-term software support still block promotion.
Research-oriented demonstration only; do not order without strong UK owner and insurer evidence.
